headline: GOLD AT $4,780 — Heads For Third Straight Weekly Gain On Dollar Weakness, US-Iran Ceasefire
snippet: Gold edged toward $4,780/oz by April 11, up 46.8% year-over-year, with the weaker dollar and US-Iran ceasefire negotiations delivering a third consecutive weekly gain. Q1 2026 average hit a record $4,873/oz.

headline: CHINA BUYS 790 Tonnes Of Silver In 8 Weeks — Solar Firms Scramble Before VAT Rebate Expires
snippet: China imported 790 tonnes of silver in January–February 2026, with 470 tonnes arriving in February alone. Solar manufacturers are stockpiling ahead of the April expiry of VAT export rebates on lithium-ion batteries.

headline: Tectonic Metals HITS 104 g/t Au Over 3.05m — Inside Wide 36.58m At 9.94 g/t At Alaska Flat Project
snippet: Tectonic Metals (TSXV:TECT) drilled a bonanza-grade hit of 104.23 g/t Au over 3.05m nested inside 36.58m at 9.94 g/t at Chicken Mountain, Flat Gold Project, Alaska. The hole ended in mineralization, confirming strong expansion potential.

headline: Dryden Gold Hits 252 g/t Au In New Sparrow Zone — THREE High-Grade Discoveries 300m From Known Gold
snippet: Dryden Gold (TSXV:DRY) found three new zones at Gold Rock, Ontario. Sparrow hit 4.25m at 32.87 g/t Au including 0.50m at 252 g/t — 300m northeast of the known Treasure and Barrelman zones on the Big Master Gold System.

headline: Founders Metals Makes FIRST Discovery At Antino North — 17.22 g/t Au In 70,000m Suriname Program
snippet: Founders Metals (TSXV:FDR) drilled 17.22 g/t Au over 3.6m within 24.6m of 2.81 g/t Au at Antino North, Suriname — the first discovery on this concession, 20km southwest of the main Upper Antino area on the 102,360-ha Guiana Shield land package.

headline: COPPER ABOVE $5.87/LB — 29% Year-Over-Year As 330,000-Tonne Supply Shortfall Takes Shape
snippet: Copper hit $5.87/lb on April 10, up 29% year-over-year. JPMorgan projects a 330,000-tonne refined shortfall in 2026, with data center construction alone set to absorb 475,000 tonnes — a structural squeeze years in the making.

headline: Coeur Mining CLOSES New Gold Deal — 392M Shares Issued, $750M Buyback, Inaugural Dividend Launched
snippet: Coeur Mining completed its all-share acquisition of New Gold on March 20, creating an all-North American senior precious metals producer guiding 680,000–815,000 oz GEO for 2026. New $750M buyback and inaugural semiannual dividend announced.

headline: Paladin POSTS Record Uranium Output Since Restart — Langer Heinrich Tracking Upper FY26 Guidance
snippet: Paladin Energy's Langer Heinrich mine produced 1.23M lbs U3O8 in Q2 FY2026, a 16% quarter-on-quarter increase. Year-to-date output of 2.30M lbs puts the Namibia mine on track for the upper end of its 4.0–4.4M lb annual guidance.

headline: URANIUM At $101/lb And Rising — U.S. DOE Commits $2.7B To Break Russian Enrichment Dependency
snippet: Spot uranium hit $101.41/lb in January 2026 on surging nuclear capacity projections. The U.S. DOE committed $2.7 billion over 10 years to expand domestic uranium enrichment, targeting independence from Russian and Central Asian suppliers.

headline: Gold Miners Eye SPRING RALLY — GDX Profits Hit Record $2,490/oz, Historical 3.1x Multiplier Awaits
snippet: Following March's brutal selloff, gold stocks face historically bullish spring seasonals. Top GDX components earned a record $2,490/oz in Q4 2025 (+106% YoY), and mid-March to early June historically delivers a 3.1x leverage to gold.

headline: Gold Stocks Down 30% From Peak — 'Triple Green Shoots' Signal REVERSAL May Already Be Underway
snippet: Despite gold near $4,800, gold mining equities have shed roughly 30% from recent highs. Three converging technical signals — breadth, momentum, and volume — are now flashing what analysts call a classic early-stage reversal setup.

headline: SILVER Faces Sixth Straight Structural Deficit — China Physical Buying Outpacing Global Mine Supply
snippet: The global silver market is on course for its sixth consecutive annual structural deficit in 2026. Money Metals Exchange notes surging Chinese industrial and investment demand is driving local prices well above international benchmarks.
